_note: TabPFN v2 (Prior Labs). CONTRACT DEVIATIONS from the TabFM engine contract, see tools/export_tabpfn: (1) train_size is NOT a scalar input — the train/test split is len(y); the engine must feed y as the TRAIN-TARGET PREFIX [1,N] (not full [1,T]). Output logits carries predictions on rows >= N (rows < N are zero pad; engine reads logits[:, train_size:]). (2) cat_mask and d are NOT graph inputs — categoricals must be ordinal-encoded to floats upstream; TabPFN auto-detects/pads features internally. (3) Do NOT z-score features on the C++ side: TabPFN standard-scales internally (feed raw). (4) Classification: logits[1,T,C], C=max_classes(10) class logits. REGRESSION: logits[1,T,1] is a SINGLE RAW-SPACE POINT ESTIMATE per row (the FullSupportBarDistribution mean over criterion.borders, de-standardized) — the graph z-normalizes the RAW train targets it is fed (population std) and maps its output back to raw space, so for regression the engine MUST feed RAW train targets and MUST NOT standardize the target nor inverse-transform the output; it reads logits[:, train_size:, 0] as the final prediction. (5) Per-task tensor maps: classification and regression are DIFFERENT graphs (the regression map adds `regression_borders` = the checkpoint's criterion.borders), so graph.tensor_map is keyed by task — the engine must resolve tensor_map[task]. (6) Multiclass target densification is disabled at export; correct when train labels are the dense set 0..C-1. (7) HF ships .ckpt (PyTorch), not safetensors — convert_weights.py converts ckpt -> safetensors keyed by the per-task tensor_map before injection. tensor_contract input names x,y match the exported graph; train_size/cat_mask/d are intentionally absent.
_weights_note: HF ships PyTorch .ckpt (pickle); the engine injects safetensors. Run once per task: `uv run python tools/export_tabpfn/convert_weights.py <classification|regression> ~/.cache/anofox-tabfm` to download + convert real weights into the cache (the regression conversion also writes `regression_borders` = criterion.borders), then predict with model := 'tabpfn-v2'. REAL-WEIGHT regression on California housing (n_train=840): MSE 0.243, R2 0.827 vs mean-baseline MSE 1.409 (82.7% reduction), ORT-vs-PyTorch parity 1.4e-5.
